tripOverview.intro: London is a vibrant metropolis where centuries of history blend seamlessly with cutting-edge culture and cuisine. From royal palaces to bustling markets, the city offers endless discoveries for romantic explorers.
tripOverview.vibe: Experience London's perfect blend of history, romance, and modern energy as you explore world-class markets, charming neighborhoods, and iconic landmarks.

destinationOverview.bestTime: April is perfect for visiting London with mild spring weather and blooming flowers in the parks.

attractions.name: Borough Market
attractions.desc: London's oldest food market with incredible artisanal vendors and global cuisine.

attractions.name: Tower Bridge
attractions.desc: Iconic Victorian bridge offering stunning views and glass floor walkways.

attractions.name: British Museum
attractions.desc: World-renowned museum housing treasures from ancient civilizations.

attractions.name: Covent Garden
attractions.desc: Historic market area filled with street performers, shops, and restaurants.

attractions.name: Westminster Abbey
attractions.desc: Gothic abbey where British monarchs have been crowned for nearly 1000 years.

destinationOverview.transportation: London's extensive Tube system connects all major attractions efficiently, with contactless payment accepted everywhere. Black cabs are iconic but expensive, while Uber and Bolt offer convenient rideshare options. Walking is often fastest for short distances, especially in central areas where attractions are clustered.
destinationOverview.tip: Get an Oyster Card or use contactless payment for seamless public transport, and always stand right on escalators to let others pass on the left.
destinationOverview.safety: London is very safe for LGBTQ+ travelers with a vibrant community and excellent emergency services.
destinationOverview.airaloText: Stay connected throughout London with affordable eSIM data plans that work seamlessly across the UK.

days.intro: Start your London adventure by getting oriented in the heart of the city.

morning.name: Arrival at Heathrow
morning.desc: Land and take the Heathrow Express to Paddington Station.

morning.name: Hotel Check-in
morning.desc: Drop bags and freshen up at your accommodation.

afternoon.name: Covent Garden Exploration
afternoon.desc: Wander through the historic market and enjoy street performers.

afternoon.name: National Gallery
afternoon.desc: Admire world-class art collections in this iconic museum.

evening.name: Dinner at Dishoom
evening.desc: Experience excellent Indian cuisine in a beautiful Bombay café setting.

evening.name: Thames Evening Stroll
evening.desc: Take a romantic walk along the South Bank with city lights.

days.tip: Book restaurant reservations in advance, especially for popular spots like Dishoom which can have long waits.

days.intro: Dive deep into London's incredible food scene and explore vibrant local areas.

morning.name: Borough Market
morning.desc: Explore London's most famous food market with amazing vegetarian options.

morning.name: Southwark Cathedral
morning.desc: Visit this beautiful Gothic cathedral near the market.

afternoon.name: Tower Bridge Exhibition
afternoon.desc: Walk across the famous glass floors and enjoy panoramic views.

afternoon.name: Shoreditch Street Art Tour
afternoon.desc: Discover incredible street art in this trendy East London neighborhood.

evening.name: Brick Lane Curry
evening.desc: Enjoy authentic Indian cuisine on London's famous curry mile.

evening.name: George Inn Historic Pub
evening.desc: Have drinks at London's last surviving galleried coaching inn.

days.tip: Visit Borough Market early to avoid crowds and get the best selection from vendors.

days.intro: Immerse yourselves in London's rich cultural offerings and romantic spots.

morning.name: British Museum
morning.desc: Explore world-famous artifacts and exhibitions at your own pace.

morning.name: Russell Square Gardens
morning.desc: Enjoy a peaceful stroll through these beautiful Bloomsbury gardens.

afternoon.name: Thames River Cruise
afternoon.desc: See London's landmarks from the water on a relaxing boat ride.

afternoon.name: Tate Modern
afternoon.desc: Discover contemporary art in this converted power station.

evening.name: Pre-theatre Dinner
evening.desc: Enjoy vegetarian fine dining at Vanilla Black near the theatre district.

evening.name: West End Show
evening.desc: Experience world-class theatre in London's famous entertainment district.

days.tip: Book West End show tickets in advance online for better prices and seat selection.

days.intro: Make the most of your final morning before heading home.

morning.name: Hyde Park Walk
morning.desc: Take a romantic morning stroll through London's most famous park.

morning.name: Last-minute Shopping
morning.desc: Pick up souvenirs at Oxford Street or Regent Street.

afternoon.name: Hotel Checkout
afternoon.desc: Collect luggage and prepare for departure.

afternoon.name: Heathrow Express
afternoon.desc: Take the fast train to the airport for your flight home.

evening.name: Flight Departure
evening.desc: Begin your journey back to Philadelphia.

days.tip: Allow extra time for airport security and arrive at Heathrow at least 2 hours before international flights.

food.intro: London's food scene brilliantly combines traditional British fare with exceptional international cuisine and outstanding vegetarian options.
food.tip: Many pubs offer excellent vegetarian options, and Indian restaurants are generally very accommodating to dietary restrictions.

neighborhoods.name: South Bank
neighborhoods.desc: Modern area near Borough Market and cultural attractions
neighborhoods.whyStay: Walking distance to major sights and food markets

neighborhoods.name: Covent Garden
neighborhoods.desc: Historic theater district with shopping and dining
neighborhoods.whyStay: Central location with easy access to West End shows

neighborhoods.name: Shoreditch
neighborhoods.desc: Trendy East London area with vibrant nightlife
neighborhoods.whyStay: Hip neighborhood with great restaurants and bars

hotels.tip: Book accommodations near Tube stations for easy access to all attractions, and consider staying south of the Thames for better value.

mustDo.name: Tower Bridge Glass Floor
mustDo.desc: Walk on transparent floors above Thames

getYourGuide.name: London Food Walking Tour
getYourGuide.desc: Guided exploration of Borough Market and local specialties with tastings included.

getYourGuide.name: Thames Speedboat Adventure
getYourGuide.desc: Thrilling high-speed boat ride through central London with commentary.

getYourGuide.name: Jack the Ripper Walking Tour
getYourGuide.desc: Evening historical tour through Victorian London's most mysterious streets.

hiddenGems.name: Leadenhall Market
hiddenGems.desc: Beautiful Victorian covered market that appeared in Harry Potter films.

hiddenGems.name: St. Dunstan in the East
hiddenGems.desc: Romantic church ruins transformed into a secret garden in the City.

hiddenGems.name: Neal's Yard
hiddenGems.desc: Colorful courtyard in Covent Garden filled with health food shops and cafes.

packing.title: April in London
packing.intro: Pack layers for mild spring weather with occasional rain showers and cool evenings.
packing.luggage: A medium suitcase plus personal carry-on should be sufficient for this 4-day trip.
packing.tip: Pack comfortable walking shoes as you'll be exploring markets and neighborhoods extensively on foot.

bookTrip.intro: Expect to pay around $600-800 per person for round-trip flights from Philadelphia to London.
